seo.meta_title: Supabase Auth vs Auth.js for Next.js App Router (2026): Which Auth Tool Wins?
seo.meta_description: Looking for Supabase Auth vs Auth.js for your Next.js app? We compare pricing (Free – $75/mo vs Free (Open Source)), DX scores, setup time, and give you our honest pick.
seo.og_title: Supabase Auth or Auth.js for Next.js? Our Honest Comparison
seo.og_description: Supabase Auth (4.4★) vs Auth.js (4.6★). Easy learning curve vs Medium. Find your ideal Next.js auth solution.

hero.headline: Supabase Auth vs Auth.js for Next.js Authentication
hero.subheadline: An honest comparison of Supabase Auth and Auth.js — pricing, DX, setup time, and which one we recommend for Next.js App Router projects in 2026.

quick_summary: When comparing Supabase Auth and Auth.js for Next.js App Router authentication, the differences come down to your priorities. Auth.js leads with a 8/10 Next.js integration score and a medium learning curve. Supabase Auth scores 7/10 with a easy learning curve. Auth.js starts at Free (Open Source), while Supabase Auth starts at Free – $75/mo.
verdict_text: We recommend Auth.js for most Next.js App Router projects. Its medium learning curve and 20 minutes setup time make it immediately productive. Supabase Auth is a worthy alternative, particularly if you need yes, 50,000 mau included in free tier or prefer Supabase Auth's approach to authentication.

structured_data_points.label: Free Tier
structured_data_points.t1: Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier

pros: Extremely generous free tier with 50,000 monthly active users
Integrated with Supabase database, storage, and realtime subscriptions
Built-in support for email, phone, OAuth, and magic links
cons: Tightly coupled to the Supabase PostgreSQL ecosystem
Less focused on auth-only use cases - it's part of a larger platform
Organization and team management features are less mature than Clerk
RLS policies can become complex to manage at scale

pros: Massive community with extensive documentation and examples
Framework-agnostic but excellent Next.js support via next-auth
Supports 80+ OAuth providers out of the box
Battle-tested in production across thousands of applications
cons: No built-in UI components - you build your own login pages
Configuration can become complex for advanced use cases
Database setup required for persistent sessions

faq.question: Should I choose Supabase Auth or Auth.js for my Next.js app?
faq.answer: It depends on your specific needs. Supabase Auth scores 7/10 for Next.js integration with a Easy learning curve. Auth.js scores 8/10 with a Medium learning curve. For most projects, we recommend Auth.js because of its stronger DX and better alignment with App Router patterns. However, if Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier or Yes, fully open source is a deciding factor, the cheaper option may serve you well.

faq.question: Is Supabase Auth or Auth.js more affordable?
faq.answer: Supabase Auth starts at Free – $75/mo with a free tier of Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier. Auth.js starts at Free (Open Source) with a free tier of Yes, fully open source. The total cost of ownership depends on your MAU (monthly active users). At lower volumes, the free tiers of both are sufficient. As you scale, Supabase Auth offers better value at scale. Factor in hidden costs like enterprise features, SSO, and database infrastructure when comparing.

faq.question: Is Supabase Auth or Auth.js easier to set up with Next.js?
faq.answer: Supabase Auth has a Easy learning curve with an estimated setup time of 15 minutes. Auth.js has a Medium learning curve with an estimated setup time of 20 minutes. Supabase Auth is faster to set up, but ease of integration also depends on your team's familiarity with the framework and whether you want pre-built UI components or prefer to build your own.

faq.question: Can I use Supabase Auth and Auth.js together?
faq.answer: Using both Supabase Auth and Auth.js in the same Next.js application is not recommended. They would create separate session management systems, conflicting middleware, and competing cookies. Choose one auth provider and stick with it. Migrating between auth providers later is possible but requires user data export and rebuilding authentication flows.

faq.question: Which has better developer experience: Supabase Auth or Auth.js?
faq.answer: Supabase Auth has a Easy learning curve, a Next.js integration score of 7/10, and a user rating of 4.4★. Auth.js has a Medium learning curve, 8/10, and 4.6★. On paper, Auth.js leads in DX. In practice, your specific requirements around pre-built components, customization, and team expertise matter just as much. Try both free tiers to see what clicks.
